nodes.title: SL:Place-BasedMidLevPracticum
nodes.description: A service-learning course designed around an immersive experience in place-based education practices with young adolescents (grades 5-9). Place-based education (PBE) is an approach to learning that takes advantage of geography to create authentic, meaningful and engaging personalized learning for students. Through a combination of on-campus seminars, field-based trips and middle level classroom teaching, participants will experience, analyze, design and teach authentic place and community-based learning experiences. Pre/Co-requisite: Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Sophomore standing. Open to all majors; Minimum Sophomore standing; On Campus & Off site school and community locations.

nodes.title: Student Teaching: Internship
nodes.description: A (15 week) full-semester, full-time, full-day, clinical component of the teacher preparation program. The primary purpose of the student teaching experience is to provide candidates with a carefully mentored experience to help develop and enhance the knowledge, skills, and dispositions necessary to positively impact student learning and development. Prerequisites: EDML 3600, EDML 4890, EDML 3700; Instructor Permission; PRAXIS Core; Earned GPA of 3. Co-requisite: EDML 4860.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor Permission; PRAXIS Core; Earned GPA of 3. EDML Seniors

nodes.title: Social Justice Teaching & Adv
nodes.description: Examines social justice issues in education and explores what it means to engage in social justice teaching and advocacy. Critically examines the roles of power, oppression, and privilege in education as students strive to envision ways to disrupt injustice and create a better, more just educational system. Prerequisite: Graduate student or Instructor permission.

nodes.title: SL:Young AdolescentELA Methods
nodes.description: Examines young adolescent literature and research-based instructional practices for supporting students with reading and writing in middle grades English Language Arts. Credit not awarded for both EDML 3770 and EDML 5770.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Middle Grades Organization
nodes.description: Contemporary middle level schools are structured specifically to respond to the unique nature of young adolescents. Explores these structures in relation to middle schoolers' developmental and cultural needs. Topics include personalization, teaming, grouping, scheduling, transition planning, and family involvement. Credit not awarded for both EDML 5700 and EDML 3700.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Middle School Org & Pedagogy
nodes.description: Focuses on exploring theory and practice in responsive school organization for young adolescents, including interdisciplinary/partner teaming, block scheduling, and teacher advisories, as well as teaching lessons in one area of specialization. Credit not awarded for both EDML 3700 and EDML 5700. Pre/co-requisite: EDML 1890, EDML 2890.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE Students

nodes.title: CL:Social Justice Education
nodes.description: Examines social justice issues in education and explores what it means to be a social justice educator. Students will critically reflect, analyze relevant cases, and engage in social justice advocacy. Pre/Co-requisites: EDML 3770 or EDML 3890 or EDML 3990 or EDTE 1610 or NR 1610 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Teaching Young Adolescents
nodes.description: Focus on understanding and reflecting on an integrative and developmental approach to the design of middle level curriculum, as well as teaching in one area of specialization.
nodes.other_requirements: Undergrad EDML majors only with minimum junior standing

nodes.title: Practicum II: Equ MG Learn Env
nodes.description: Teaching practicum on middle level team in one of two areas of academic concentration, acquiring knowledge of and skills in curriculum, pedagogy, and assessment. Prerequisite: Admission to Middle Level Professional program. Pre/Co-requisite: Admission to Middle Level Professional Program.
nodes.other_requirements: Admission to Middle Level Professional program; EDML majors; senior standing; course fee: $40.00

nodes.title: CL:Foundations of PBE
nodes.description: Introduces the principles and practices of place-based education. Students learn to design place-based curriculum and educative materials from an interdisciplinary analysis of specific places. Cross-listed with: SEP 1610.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor Permission Required; Priority registration for students enrolled in PBE certificate. Then open to Degree & PACE students; Cross listed with SEP 1610A

nodes.title: Teachers & Teaching Process
nodes.description: Examines professional responsibilities of middle level teachers as defined by Vermont and national standards via classroom observations.

nodes.title: Middle Grades Integ Curr & Ped
nodes.description: Applies frameworks to design culturally responsive, developmentally appropriate curriculum and assessments for young adolescents. Drawing on Successful Middle Schools (Bishop & Harrison), focuses on applying project-based, place-based, and personalized learning approaches to creating units that are challenging, exploratory, integrative, and diverse. Course is aligned with the Vermont Middle Grades Endorsement. Prerequisite: Graduate student or Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Equitable MG Learning Environ
nodes.description: Provides a school-based teaching and learning experience that will continue into student teaching in the following term. Through internship and weekly course meetings, provides an opportunity to design and engage in practices that are appropriate for the diverse experiences and identities of young adolescent learners. Credit not awarded for both EDML 5890 and EDML 3890.

nodes.title: ML Internship Seminar
nodes.description: Supports students engaged in their student teaching semester. Develop, share, and critically evaluate portfolio work. Process challenges experienced in the field. Credit not awarded for both EDML 5860 and EDML 4860. Prerequisite: EDML 5992.
nodes.other_requirements: EDML Seniors & MAT students; Take concurrently with ML Student Teaching:EDML 4991 or 6991.

nodes.title: Internship: Student Teaching
nodes.description: A full-semester, full-time, full-day, clinical component of the teacher preparation program. Provides students with a carefully mentored experience to help develop and enhance the knowledge, skills, and dispositions necessary to positively impact student learning and development. Prerequisites: EDML 6070, EDML 5890.
nodes.other_requirements: EDML MAT Students Only; Location: Off site at various schools; Take concurrently with Student Teaching SeminarEDML 5830
